title: Buttery Crisp Japanese Freekeh Fried Rice
description: A post-workout Japanese dish featuring freekeh, with a buttery and crisp texture, halal-friendly. This recipe combines the nutty flavor of freekeh with the savory taste of Japanese seasonings.
instructions: Rinse the freekeh and cook according to package instructions. Set aside.
Heat 1 tablespoon of cooking oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the cooked freekeh to the skillet or wok, stirring to combine with the onion and garlic.
Add the soy sauce and mirin, stirring to combine. Cook for 2-3 minutes, until the freekeh is lightly toasted.
Push the freekeh mixture to one side of the skillet or wok. Add the unsalted butter to the other side and let melt.
Once the butter has melted, mix it with the freekeh mixture, stirring to combine.
Add the chopped scallions and sesame seeds, stirring to combine. Cook for an additional minute, until the scallions are lightly toasted.
Season with salt to taste, then serve hot.
